segments.text: "Agrippa then said to Paul,
You are free to speak on your own behalf.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: With a sweep of his arm, Paul began his defense.
I am delighted, King Agrippa, to make my defense before you today regarding everything I am accused of by the Jews,
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: particularly because you are an expert in all Jewish issues and customs. I beg your patient indulgence as you listen to what I have to say.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: All the Jews know my life story—from my earliest days beginning in my own country and then in Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: They have known me for a long time and can verify, if they choose to, that I have followed the religious school that observes our faith in the strictest way—I lived as a Pharisee.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: Now I am standing here to be judged regarding the promised hope God gave to our fathers
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: that our twelve tribes hoped to receive as they continually dedicated themselves in God's service. Yes, it's because of this hope that I'm accused by the Jews, Your Majesty!
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: Why should any of you think it's unbelievable that God raises the dead?
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: Previously I was sincerely convinced I should do as much as I could to oppose the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: This is what I did in Jerusalem. I threw many of the believers in prison, having been given authority to do this by the chief priests. When they were sentenced to death I cast my vote against them.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: I had them punished in all the synagogues, trying to make them recant. I was so furiously opposed to them that I went to cities outside our country to persecute them.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: That's why one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and orders from the chief priests.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: At about noon as I was on my way, Your Majesty, I saw a light from heaven that blazed brighter than the sun. It shone around me and those who were traveling with me.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: All of us fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice speaking to me in Aramaic, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me? It's hard for you to fight against me!’
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: ‘Who are you, Lord?’ I asked. ‘I am Jesus, the one you're persecuting,’ the Lord replied.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: ‘But pick yourself up and get to your feet. The reason why I've appeared to you is to appoint you as my servant, to be a witness for me, telling others how you have seen me and everything I will reveal to you.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: I will save you from your own people and from the foreigners. I am sending you to them
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: to open their eyes so they can turn from darkness to light, from the power of Satan to God, and so they can receive forgiveness for their sins and a place with those who are set right as they trust in me.’
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: Clearly, King Agrippa, I could not disobey this vision from heaven.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: First in Damascus, then in Jerusalem, and then all over Judea and also to the foreigners I shared the message of repentance: how they should turn to God, demonstrating their repentance through their actions.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: That's why the Jews seized me in the Temple and tried to kill me.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: God has looked after me so I can st and here today as a witness to everyone, both to ordinary people and to those who are important. I am only repeating what Moses and the prophets said would happen—
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: how the Messiah had to suffer, and that by being the first to rise from the dead he would announce the light of God's salvation to both Jews and foreigners.
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: "Festus interrupted Paul as he made his defense, shouting out,
Paul, you've gone mad! All your knowledge is driving you insane!
segments.speaker: Agrippa then

segments.text: I'm not mad, Festus your Excellency,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Paul replied.
What I am saying is true and makes sense.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: The king recognizes this, and I'm explaining it very clearly. I am sure that he is aware of what's been happening, because none of this took place as if it were hidden in a corner.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: King Agrippa, do you believe what the prophets said? I'm sure you do!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Do you think you can convince me to become a Christian so quickly?
segments.speaker: Agrippa

segments.text: Agrippa asked Paul.
Whether it takes a short time or a long time doesn't matter,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Paul answered.
But my prayer to God is that not just you, but everybody listening to me today would become like me—except for these chains!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: "The king stood up, along with the governor and Bernice, and everyone who had been sitting with them.
"They conferred together after they had left.
This man hasn't done anything that deserves death or imprisonment,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: they concluded.
"Agrippa told Festus,
He could have been freed if he hadn't appealed to Caesar.
segments.speaker: Agrippa
